task.name: Create shared formatCurrency utility
task.action: Create a new file `src/lib/format.ts` with a named export `formatCurrency` function that formats a number using `toLocaleString('en-PH', { minimumFractionDigits: 2, maximumFractionDigits: 2 })`.
task.verify: Run `npm run build` to confirm the new file compiles. Import it in a throwaway check or just verify no type errors.

task.name: Expose refetch from useNetWorth hook
task.action: Edit `src/hooks/useNetWorth.ts`. Destructure `refetch` from the `useQuery` return value alongside `data`, `isPending`, and `error`. Add `refetch` to the hook's return object.
task.verify: Run `npm run build` to confirm no type errors. The hook's return type should now include `refetch`.

task.name: Add animated count-up for net worth value
task.action: Edit `src/components/dashboard/TotalNetWorthCard.tsx`: 1. Import `useSpring`, `useTransform`, `motion`, and `useReducedMotion` from `framer-motion`. 2. Import `formatCurrency` from `@/lib/format` and remove the inline `formatCurrency` function. 3. Create a spring animation: `useSpring(0, { duration: 800, bounce: 0 })` that targets the `netWorth` value. 4. Use `useTransform` to pipe the spring value through `formatCurrency` for display. 5. Use `useReducedMotion()` — if true, skip the animation and display the formatted value directly. 6. Use a `useEffect` to set the spring target to `netWorth` when data loads or changes. 7. Render the animated value using `` or a regular span that reads the transformed value.
task.verify: Run `npm run build`. Visually verify the net worth value animates from 0 on page load. Toggle `prefers-reduced-motion` in browser DevTools and confirm the value appears instantly.

task.name: Restyle monthly change as pill badge
task.action: Edit `src/components/dashboard/TotalNetWorthCard.tsx`: 1. Wrap the monthly change indicator in a pill container with classes: `flex items-center gap-1 px-2.5 py-1 rounded-full text-xs md:text-sm font-medium`. 2. Add background tint based on direction: - Positive: `bg-text-success/10 text-text-success` - Negative: `bg-text-error/10 text-text-error` - Neutral: `bg-secondary-400/10 text-secondary-400` 3. Remove the `{isPositive && '+'}{isNegative && '-'}` text prefixes — the arrow icon already indicates direction. 4. Keep the format: `[ArrowIcon] ₱{amount} ({percentage}%)`
task.verify: Run `npm run build`. Visually verify the monthly change now appears as a colored pill badge with tinted background.

task.name: Add balance privacy eye toggle
task.action: Edit `src/components/dashboard/TotalNetWorthCard.tsx`: 1. Import `Eye` and `EyeOff` from `lucide-react`. 2. Add a `useState` boolean `isBalanceHidden` (default `false`). 3. Next to the "Total Net Worth" label, add a button with the Eye/EyeOff icon that toggles `isBalanceHidden`. 4. Style the button: `cursor-pointer p-1 rounded-md hover:bg-secondary-700 transition-colors` with `aria-label="Toggle balance visibility"`. 5. When `isBalanceHidden` is true: - Replace the net worth value with `*****` - Replace the monthly change amount and percentage with `***` 6. The toggle should not affect the loading or error states.
task.verify: Run `npm run build`. Visually verify clicking the eye icon toggles between showing the real balance and `*****`. Check that the button has a visible hover state and correct cursor.

task.name: Improve error state with retry button
task.action: Edit `src/components/dashboard/TotalNetWorthCard.tsx`: 1. Import `AlertCircle` from `lucide-react`. 2. Destructure `refetch` from `useNetWorth()` (added in Phase 1). 3. Replace the current error state with: - An `AlertCircle` icon (size `h-8 w-8 text-error-600`) - The "Failed to load net worth" message - The error detail message - A "Try again" button styled with `cursor-pointer text-sm font-medium text-primary hover:text-primary/80 transition-colors` that calls `refetch()` on click.
task.verify: Run `npm run build`. To test: temporarily break the API endpoint or mock an error. The error state should show the icon, message, and a clickable "Try again" button.

task.name: Convert to named exports
task.action: 1. Edit `src/components/dashboard/TotalNetWorthCard.tsx`: Change `export default TotalNetWorthCard` to `export { TotalNetWorthCard }`. 2. Edit `src/components/dashboard/NetWorthDisplay.tsx`: Update the import from `import TotalNetWorthCard from './TotalNetWorthCard'` to `import { TotalNetWorthCard } from './TotalNetWorthCard'`. 3. Edit `src/components/dashboard/NetWorthDisplay.tsx`: Change `export default NetWorthDisplay` to `export { NetWorthDisplay }`. 4. Search for any other imports of `NetWorthDisplay` and update them to use the named import.
task.verify: Run `npm run build` to confirm all imports resolve correctly with zero errors.

task.name: Update skeleton loading state
task.action: Edit `src/components/dashboard/TotalNetWorthCard.tsx`: 1. In the loading skeleton, replace the monthly change skeleton with a pill-shaped skeleton: `Skeleton` with classes `h-6 w-28 md:w-36 rounded-full bg-secondary-500` to match the new pill badge shape. 2. Ensure the skeleton layout mirrors the real content layout (title row with pill on right, value below).
task.verify: Run `npm run build`. Visually verify the loading skeleton matches the shape of the real content — especially the pill-shaped badge placeholder.

task.name: Build, Lint, and Final Check
task.action: Run the following commands sequentially: 1. `npm run lint` — must pass with zero errors 2. `npm run build` — must compile with zero errors 3. Verify no regressions: the dashboard should load correctly with the animated net worth, pill badge, eye toggle, and improved error state.
task.verify: All commands exit with code 0. Dashboard renders correctly with all new features.
